query: Sichuan kung pao chicken with peanuts, dried chilies, and savory sweet sour sauce

draft.title: Kung Pao Chicken
draft.description: Sichuan kung pao chicken with peanuts, dried chilies, and savory sweet sour sauce, perfect for home cooking in Europe.

ingredients.name: spring onion
ingredients.note: white and green parts separated, sliced

steps.text: Mix chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp cornstarch, and Shaoxing wine. Let marinate 10 minutes.
Prepare the sauce by mixing remaining soy sauces, vinegar, sugar, water, and 1 tsp cornstarch until smooth.
Heat peanut oil in a wok or large pan over medium-high heat. Fry dried chilies until fragrant but not burnt, then remove and set aside.
Add marinated chicken to the wok and stir-fry until cooked through and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and white parts of spring onion; stir-fry for 1 minute until aromatic.
Pour in the prepared sauce and cook, stirring, until it thickens and coats the chicken.
Return dried chilies to the wok along with roasted peanuts; toss everything together.
Remove from heat and garnish with green parts of spring onion before serving with steamed rice.

warnings: Adjust dried chili quantity to your spice tolerance.
Use fresh peanuts or roasted without salt for best flavor.
